## Summary

I've implemented `Bun.INI` — a new format namespace with `parse` and `stringify` methods — following the exact same pattern as `Bun.TOML`, `Bun.JSONC`, `Bun.JSON5`, and `Bun.YAML`.

### Files created

**`src/bun.js/api/INIObject.zig`** — Self-contained INI parser and stringifier (no external INI library used):
- **`parse(input)`** — Splits the input by newlines, skips empty lines and full-line comments (`;`/`#`), treats `[section]` headers as nested objects, and coerces values to their JS types (`true`/`false` → boolean, `null` → null, numeric strings → number, quoted strings → stripped string, everything else → string). Inline comments are stripped from unquoted values. Prototype pollution via `__proto__` keys/sections is prevented.
- **`stringify(obj)`** — Two-pass approach: first emits top-level scalars as plain `key = value` lines, then emits top-level objects as `[section]` blocks. Arrays emit one `key = value` line per element. All string values are JSON-quoted to ensure unambiguous round-trips.

**`test/js/bun/ini.test.ts`** — Comprehensive test suite covering parse coercion, sections, comments, inline comments, Windows line endings, duplicate keys/sections, prototype pollution prevention, stringify, and roundtrip correctness.
